identity.name: yexca
identity.description: This is yexca's personal blog: AI workflows, local automation, image recognition, data processing, multilingual notes, and the process of turning repeated small problems into practical tools.

items.title: AI and data experiments
items.text: Image recognition / YOLO / data preparation / media quality checks

items.title: Multilingual knowledge systems
items.text: Hugo blog / translation tooling / VRChat docs / personal services

cards.title: Repeated Problems Become Tools
cards.text: If a tiny annoyance appears often enough, it deserves a script, a workflow, or a small app.

cards.title: AI Workflows Need Pipelines
cards.text: Models are only one part of the work; preparation, checks, slicing, archiving, and deployment matter too.

cards.title: Local First, Inspectable
cards.text: I like tools that are easy to run, easy to inspect, and still useful after the first experiment.

cards.title: Notes Become Systems
cards.text: The blog, translation tooling, VRChat docs, and project logs are all parts of one maintained knowledge system.

items.name: PixivDownloader-WebUI
items.desc: A local WebUI for Pixiv backups, queues, metadata, and browsing.

items.name: AI-Cover-Tools
items.desc: Audio separation, slicing, and checks for AI cover training material.

items.name: zeroclaw-dockyard
items.desc: A Docker-first control panel for multiple local ZeroClaw agents.

items.name: yasumi-project-deploy
items.desc: Deployment entry point for the Yasumi personal planning system.

items.name: Kumo
items.desc: A lightweight self-hosted image hosting service for personal workflows.

items.name: kikoto
items.desc: A local media library for organizing voice works and related metadata.

history_section.title: From problem notes to a maintained knowledge base

items.label: Start
items.title: A Place to Write Things Down
items.body: The blog began as a small place for learning notes, fixes, and experiments that I did not want to lose.

items.label: Sakurairo
items.title: Learning by Changing Themes
items.body: Early theme changes were not just decoration. They were how I learned what kind of site felt like mine.

items.label: Argon
items.title: A Blog That Could Stay Around
items.body: Argon made the site feel practical enough for long-term writing, problem solving, and daily maintenance.

items.label: Docker
items.title: Deployment Became Reproducible
items.body: Docker turned server setup from memory work into something closer to infrastructure notes I could repeat.

items.label: Jekyll
items.title: Toward Static Generation
items.body: Moving to Jekyll shifted the blog from a themed website toward a lighter static publishing workflow.

items.label: Hugo
items.title: A Multilingual Knowledge Base
items.body: Hugo brought internationalization, translation workflow, and a more maintainable structure for notes across languages.

items.label: Counter
items.title: A Small Sign of Life
items.body: The counter is tiny, but it makes the page feel visited, alive, and a little more personal.

items.label: Refactor
items.title: Introducing the Blog Again
items.body: The Stack theme refactor became a chance to rethink what this blog says about me now: code, tools, notes, and life.

footer: Every experience is a commit. Stay curious, and keep exploring.
